steps.title: Find c from the given condition p(c) = 10
steps.content:
Substitute $x = c$ into the function definition: $$p(c) = \frac{(c-c)^2 + 180}{2c} = \frac{0 + 180}{2c} = \frac{90}{c}$$ Since we know $p(c) = 10$, set up the equation: $$\frac{90}{c} = 10$$ Multiply both sides by $c$ and divide by 10: $$90 = 10c$$
$$c = 9$$

steps.title: Calculate p(15) using c = 9
steps.content: Now substitute $x = 15$ and $c = 9$ into the original function: $$p(15) = \frac{(15-9)^2 + 180}{2(9)}$$ Simplify the numerator: $$p(15) = \frac{6^2 + 180}{18} = \frac{36 + 180}{18} = \frac{216}{18} = 12$$ That matches Choice D — 12.00.
